regions.us-ports: Miami remains the lead US cruise homeport today with clear skies, SSE winds at 6 knots, and less than 10% rain probability supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions. Port Everglades and Port Canaveral will see SSE winds increasing to 14 knots with overcast skies today, but rain remains unlikely with less than 10% rain probability, so operations should remain normal. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, Miami and Tampa Bay will experience SSE winds near 9 knots accompanied by rain showers, though less than 10% rain probability suggests no significant weather impacts for cruise activities. Beyond 48 hours, Miami’s SSE winds pick up to 12 knots with overcast skies and a slightly elevated 21% rain probability, so passengers should stay alert for minor changes but no major disruptions are expected. Jacksonville and other ports along the US East Coast remain clear of any operational weather concerns during this period.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, we have easterly-southeast winds at 6 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability in Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, and surrounding Bahamas ports, so port operations and shore excursions will proceed smoothly with minimal weather disruptions. Looking ahead to the next 24-48 hours, expect southeast winds increasing to 11-12 knots with rain showers and overcast skies but still less than 10% rain probability across Nassau, Key West, Grand Cayman, and the northern Dominican Republic ports like Puerto Plata and Santo Domingo, which should keep operations normal. By day three, winds will strengthen to 14 knots from the southeast with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability in Nassau and Samaná, requiring only minor adjustments for outdoor activities. Visibility remains good throughout, and sea conditions are expected to stay favorable for safe embarkation and disembarkation across all Central Caribbean and Bahamas ports. Passengers and crew should monitor updates but can plan for mostly clear sailing and port visits through the period.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, an east wind at 15 knots and partly cloudy skies with a 45% rain probability may affect shore excursions and port operations in San Juan and St. Thomas, so plan accordingly for intermittent showers. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, sustained east winds near 13 knots combined with drizzle and a 53% rain probability could impact St. Croix, St. Maarten, and Antigua, potentially causing delays during embarkation and disembarkation. By day three, east winds increasing to 16 knots with drizzle and a 61% rain probability are expected to affect St. Kitts and Antigua, likely impacting outdoor activities and requiring operational adjustments. Visibility should remain generally good but keep an eye on localized reductions during drizzle periods across all ports. Crew and passengers should remain prepared for wet conditions and moderate east winds through this period.
